question_text: A student compares ultrasound waves (frequency 40 000 Hz) with normal hearing sound (frequency 5000 Hz). Both travel through air at the same speed of 330 m/s. Which statement correctly compares these waves?

options.label: A
options.text: The ultrasound has a longer wavelength and a higher pitch than the hearing sound.

options.label: B
options.text: The ultrasound has a shorter wavelength and a higher pitch than the hearing sound.

options.label: C
options.text: The ultrasound has a shorter wavelength but lower pitch than the hearing sound.

options.label: D
options.text: The ultrasound has the same wavelength as the hearing sound because they travel at the same speed.

explanation: Using wave speed = frequency × wavelength, the ultrasound with higher frequency must have a shorter wavelength (since speed is constant). Higher frequency also means higher pitch. You can calculate: ultrasound wavelength = 330 ÷ 40,000 = 0.00825 m, while hearing sound wavelength = 330 ÷ 5000 = 0.066 m, confirming the ultrasound wavelength is much shorter.
